title: Migration Gone Wrong
summary: A schema migration in the latest release ran halfway and aborted, leaving the orders table in an inconsistent state. New orders are failing to save and the on-call DBA wants you to drive the recovery before anyone touches the database again.

persona.name: Raj Patel
persona.description: A meticulous database lead who is calm precisely because the situation is dangerous. Insists on stopping writes and confirming a backup before any remediation. Loses confidence in anyone who proposes 'just re-run the migration' without first protecting the data. Reassured by careful, reversible steps and explicit checkpoints.

stakes: The orders table backs all revenue; a careless fix could permanently corrupt order history, and every minute new orders fail is direct lost sales during peak hours.
hidden_facts: The migration added a non-null column without a default, so inserts that omit it now fail.
A clean backup was taken automatically right before the deploy and is restorable.
Adding a temporary default to the new column unblocks new orders immediately without a restore.
The half-applied migration is recorded as 'failed' but partially present, so naively re-running it will error.
objectives: Pause new writes or acknowledge the failing inserts before remediating
Confirm a known-good backup exists before changing anything
Identify the non-null-without-default column as the cause and add a temporary default
Sequence a safe path that avoids blindly re-running the half-applied migration
opening_line: The orders table is half-migrated and inserts are failing. Before anyone runs anything else — walk me through your recovery plan, step by step.
